key_insight: A deep learning pipeline can extract tumor microenvironment features from multiplexed DLBCL images and distinguish GCB from ABC/non-GCB phenotypes by differences in cell composition and spatial interactions.
clinical_relevance: These microenvironment-based features may improve risk stratification beyond the Hans classifier and help refine patient subgroup characterization in diffuse large B-cell lymphoma.
method: Unbiased deep learning analysis of multiplexed tumor images
Quantification of cell composition, morphology, and spatial organization
Comparison of microenvironment patterns between DLBCL subtypes
evidence_signal: In a DLBCL patient cohort, the approach identified subtype-associated shifts, including higher immune cell proportions in ABC tumors and greater ABC interaction with M2 macrophages.
watch_item: This is a characterization study, so further validation is needed before microenvironment features can be used as a routine clinical stratification tool.
paper_title: Characterization of DLBCL cell of origin-phenotypes based on tumor microenvironment features
